entries.name: BooleanTrueChance
entries.notes: Number from 0.0 to 100.0 used for the chance to return true.

entries.name: HashSalt
entries.notes: Represents a salt value used in hashing.

entries.name: SortComparisonNumber
entries.notes:
A number that is the result of comparison two items.

In "ascending" order, a value:
- smaller than another will return -1 (or less).
- equal to another will return 0
- greater than another will return 1 (or more).
- Example: a - b

In "descending" order, this returns the opposite values as "ascending".
- Example: b - a

entries.name: BooleanStringKey
entries.notes: A string type used as a key in boolean arrays.

entries.name: RangeInputEndValue
entries.notes: Exclusive end value used by range().

entries.name: AuthClaimKey
entries.notes: Key in the claims.

entries.name: AuthRole
entries.notes:
An application role.

Is used in the client to decide which content a user can view.

Roles should always be lowercase.

entries.name: EmailAddressDomain
entries.notes: Domain name portion of an email address (e.g., "example.com").

entries.name: EmailAddress
entries.notes: Type representing an email address as a string.

entries.name: EmailParticipantString
entries.notes: Email participant string. Starts with the email, followed by the name if available.

entries.name: PhoneExtensionNumber
entries.notes: 1-6 digit extension number

entries.name: ReadableTimeString
entries.notes:
Represents a string for a time. This may be human-input, and
can be interpreted in various ways depending on the input.

Examples:
- 1:20AM
- 1:20
- 120AM
- 120

entries.name: RFC3339DateString
entries.notes:
A valid RFC3339 formatted date string.

I.E. "2020-04-30 00:00:00.000Z" and "2020-04-30T00:00:00.000Z"

The only difference between this and an ISO8601DateString is the spacing between the date and time is allowed, while in ISO8601DateString it is not.

entries.name: ISO8601DateString
entries.notes:
A valid ISO8601 formatted date string.

I.E. "2020-04-30T00:00:00.000Z"

entries.name: UTCDateString
entries.notes:
A UTC date string.

I.E. "Sat, 03 Feb 2001 04:05:06 GMT"

entries.name: ISO8601DateStringUTCFull
entries.notes:
A full ISO8601 date string that is in UTC.

I.E. "2020-04-30T00:00:00.000Z"

entries.name: TimezoneString
entries.notes:
A valid timezone string.

I.E. "UTC", "America/Denver", etc.

entries.name: TimezoneAbbreviation
entries.notes: A timezone abbreviation (UTC, EST, etc).

entries.name: ISO8601DayString
entries.notes:
A full date string Formatted as ISO8601 with 4 digits for the year.

Year, Month, Day

I.E. 1921-06-23

NOTE: Negative years and years with more than 4 digits are not supported/expected. Support can be added later, but will require adding a more complex regex, and improved parsing in

entries.name: MonthDaySlashDate
entries.notes: Date that is represented by slashes. Is considered in the Month/Day/Year format.

entries.name: UnixDateTimeMillisecondsNumber
entries.notes:
Time in milliseconds since the epoch.

Returned by Date.getTime().

entries.name: Milliseconds
entries.notes: Number of milliseconds.

entries.name: Seconds
entries.notes: Number of seconds.

entries.name: Minutes
entries.notes: Number of minutes.

entries.name: Hours
entries.notes: Number of hours.

entries.name: Days
entries.notes: Number of days.

entries.name: DayOfMonth
entries.notes: Day of the month, 1-31

entries.name: MonthOfYear
entries.notes:
Month of the year, 1-12.

NOTE: The month from Date.getMonth() is from 0-11. Use monthOfYearFromDate() to get the MonthOfYear value.

entries.name: DateMonth
entries.notes: Javascript Date month number. 0-11.

entries.name: YearNumber
entries.notes: Year number. I.E. 2022

entries.name: UnixDateTimeSecondsNumber
entries.notes:
This value is in seconds since the Epoch.

Use UnixDateTimeMillisecondsNumber instead if you need milliseconds.

entries.name: FractionalHour
entries.notes:
A number that represents hours and rounded to the nearest minute.

It has only three decimal places max.

Fractional hours are safe to add together.

For example:

- 10 minutes: 0.16
- 15 minutes : 0.25

entries.name: MinuteOfDay
entries.notes:
The minute of the day.

Number from 0-1439.

entries.name: EncryptedString
entries.notes:
Branded type representing a string that has been encrypted.

Use this type to distinguish encrypted ciphertext from plain strings at the type level.
The actual encryption algorithm is determined by the {@link StringEncryptionProvider}.

entries.name: StringErrorCode
entries.notes: A unique identifier for a specific error.

entries.name: FileSize
entries.notes: File size in bytes.

entries.name: XMLSVG
entries.notes: String value containing an XML SVG string.

entries.name: ModelKey
entries.notes: A string model key

entries.name: ModelId
entries.notes: A string model identifier (typically the document/record ID segment, not the full path).

entries.name: ModelTypeString
entries.notes: Arbitrary model type.

entries.name: TypedModelKey
entries.notes: String model key from which the model's type can be inferred from.

entries.name: ModelKeyTypePairString
entries.notes: An encoded ModelKeyTypePair.

entries.name: BitwiseEncodedSetIndex
entries.notes: Enum or number value from 0 to 31 that denotes a specific item via index of the bit to measure.

entries.name: BitwiseEncodedSet
entries.notes:
Set of values that are encoded into a single number.

The number can include up to 32 unique boolean values.

entries.name: WholeDollarAmount
entries.notes: Whole dollar amounts, before the ','.

entries.name: DollarAmount
entries.notes: Dollar amount number.

entries.name: DollarAmountPerHour
entries.notes: Dollars amount number per hour.

entries.name: DollarAmountPerDay
entries.notes: Dollars amount number per day.

entries.name: CentsAmount
entries.notes: Amount in whole cents.

entries.name: DollarAmountUnit
entries.notes: Unit to affix to a dollar amount string.

entries.name: DollarAmountString
entries.notes:
String representing a dollar amount.

Is formatted as a number with two decimal places. No unit is affixed.

entries.name: Radix36EncodedNumber

examples.code:
const encoded: Radix36EncodedNumber = 'z'; // represents 35
const largeEncoded: Radix36EncodedNumber = '2s'; // represents 100

entries.notes:
A number encoded as a radix-36 string using digits 0-9 and letters a-z.

Radix-36 encoding produces compact string representations of numbers,
useful for URL-safe identifiers and short codes.

entries.name: HexString
entries.notes: A string containing only hexadecimal characters (0-9, a-f or A-F).

entries.name: Count
entries.notes: A non-negative integer representing a count of items.

entries.name: NumberString
entries.notes: A string represented within a number.

entries.name: PercentNumber
entries.notes:
Number that represents a percent.

 e.g. PercentNumber of 5 = 0.05 = 5%

entries.name: PercentDecimal
entries.notes:
A percent decimal value.

e.g. PercentDecimal of 0.05 = 5%

entries.name: PayRate
entries.notes: Rate per hour the worker should be paid.

entries.name: PayAmount
entries.notes: The total pay amount that should be paid.

entries.name: NumberPrecision
entries.notes: The number of decimal places ot use.

entries.name: StepNumber
entries.notes: Number used to increase or decrease by the "step" value. When used in rounding the steps are aligned at a StepOrigin.

entries.name: StepOrigin
entries.notes: Origin value for StepNumbers that is used as an offset for input. Is usually 0.

entries.name: ObjectKey
entries.notes: String key of an object.

entries.name: PageNumber
entries.notes: Numeric page identifier.

entries.name: SlashPathTypedFileExtension
entries.notes:
The file extension of a SlashPathTypedFile.

e.g. 'png' in 'image.png'

entries.name: SlashPathUntypedFile
entries.notes: A SlashPath file name without a file type identifier (e.g. 'image', and not 'image.png')

entries.name: PerformTasksInParallelTaskUniqueKey
entries.notes: Used as a key to identify the "group" that a task belongs to to prevent other concurrent tasks from that group from running in parallel when parallel execution is desired.

entries.name: RelationString
entries.notes: A string-based relation identifier.

entries.name: RelationModelType
entries.notes: A string identifying the type/category of a relation model.

entries.name: StoredDataString
entries.notes: String representation of data that is stored.

entries.name: StoredDataStorageKey
entries.notes: Key used for accessing stored data.

entries.name: NumberStringDencoderNumber
entries.notes: An encodable number. This is typically a positive integer value.

entries.name: NumberStringDencoderString
entries.notes: A number-encoded string. Little-Endian. Should Decode to the same value each time.

entries.name: NumberStringDencoderDigits
entries.notes:
Digits used when encoding/decoding a value.

The number of digits/characters must be a factor of 2. I.E. 8, 16, 32, 64

entries.name: CssClass
entries.notes: Represents a single CSS Class

entries.name: CssTokenName
entries.notes: The name portion of a CSS token, without the leading `--` prefix.

entries.name: CssStyle
entries.notes: Represents a single CSS Style

entries.name: SpaceSeparatedCssClasses
entries.notes: Represents one or more CssClasses that are space separated.

entries.name: SpaceSeparatedCssStyles
entries.notes: Represents one or more CssStyles that are space separated.

entries.name: JSONEncodedString
entries.notes: A json encoded string.

entries.name: MimeTypeWithoutParameters
entries.notes:
A simple mime type string with just the type/subtype and no parameters.

I.E. "application/json"

entries.name: MimeTypeWithSubtypeWildcardWithoutParameters
entries.notes:
A mime type with a wildcard subtype. Has a star as the subtype.

I.E. "application/*"

entries.name: ContentTypeMimeType
entries.notes:
A mime type string that may contain additional parameters.

I.E. "text/plain", "application/json; charset=utf-8"

entries.name: PasswordString
entries.notes: A password string

entries.name: CharacterPrefix
entries.notes:
A character or string that is added to the start of a string.

Example: '-' in "-class"

entries.name: CharacterSuffix
entries.notes:
A character or string that is added to the end of a string.

Example: '-' in "class-"

entries.name: CharacterPrefixSuffixCleanString
entries.notes: A string that does not have a CharacterPrefix or CharacterSuffix.

entries.name: SplitStringTreeNodeString
entries.notes: A string value representing a node in a split string tree.

entries.name: WebsiteProtocol
entries.notes:
Connection protocol

I.E. http, https, etc.

entries.name: WebsiteDomain
entries.notes:
A website domain.

Examples:
- dereekb.com
- components.dereekb.com

entries.name: WebsiteTopLevelDomain
entries.notes:
The top-level domain (tld).

Examples:
- com
- net

entries.name: PortNumber
entries.notes:
A connection port number.

Example:
- 443
- 8080

entries.name: WebsiteUrl
entries.notes:
A website url. Is at minimum a domain.

Examples:
- dereekb.com
- https://components.dereekb.com
- https://components.dereekb.com/
- https://components.dereekb.com/doc/home?
- https://components.dereekb.com/doc/home?test=true&test2=true

entries.name: WebsiteUrlWithPrefix
entries.notes: A website url that starts with the proper http/https prefix.

entries.name: StandardInternetAccessibleWebsiteUrl
entries.notes:
A "standard" internet accessible website url with a tld (.com/.net/etc) and optionally has the http/https protocol, but no other protocol.

Examples:
- dereekb.com
- dereekb.com:8080
- components.dereekb.com/
- components.dereekb.com/doc/home
- http://dereekb.com
- https://components.dereekb.com/
- https://components.dereekb.com/doc/home

Non-examples:
- localhost:8080       // not internet-accessible
- test://dereekb.com   // non-http/https protocol

entries.name: WebsiteDomainAndPath
entries.notes:
A website's domain and path combined, without the BaseWebsiteUrl

Examples:
- dereekb.com
- components.dereekb.com/
- components.dereekb.com/doc/home

entries.name: CityString
entries.notes: City name string (e.g. "San Antonio").

entries.name: StateString
entries.notes: Full state name string (e.g. "Texas").

entries.name: StateCodeString
entries.notes: Two-letter US state code (e.g. "TX").

entries.name: CountryString
entries.notes: Full country name string (e.g. "United States").

entries.name: CountryCodeString
entries.notes: Country code string (e.g. "US").

entries.name: AddressLineString
entries.notes: A single line in a street address.

entries.name: ZipCodeString
entries.notes: Postal/zip code string.

entries.name: CronExpression
entries.notes: A cron schedule expression string

entries.name: IndexNumber
entries.notes:
An integer that denotes which index an item is at.

Is typically non-negative only.

entries.name: ModifierKey
entries.notes: String key that uniquely identifies a modifier within a {@link ModifierMap}.

entries.name: Pixels
entries.notes: Pixel count.

entries.name: PixelsString
entries.notes: Pixel count as a string with a "px" suffix (e.g. "100px").

entries.name: Latitude
entries.notes: Latitude value in degrees, ranging from -90 to 90.

entries.name: Longitude
entries.notes: Longitude value in degrees, ranging from -180 to 180.

entries.name: LatLngPoint
entries.notes: A geographic point represented by latitude and longitude.

entries.name: ZoomLevel
entries.notes: Camera zoom level.
